messages.content:
Through sources, permissions, and expectations, you’re taking a close look at the contacts lists that you’re sending email to. This will help you ______.

build trust

create segments

build segmentation

build email deliverability
build email deliverability

What are the two buckets that your post-send metrics fall into?

Segmented and non-segmented

Opened and clicked

Positive and negative

Engaged and did not engage
Engaged and did not engage.

When sending emails and setting goals, you want to go beyond checkpoints of activities for your goals. What are examples of checkpoints of activity?

SMART goals

Segmentation

Engagement

Open and click rates

Open and click rates are examples of checkpoints of activity. These metrics show whether your subscribers are opening and interacting with your emails, but do not necessarily indicate whether your overall goals are being achieved. SMART goals and segmentation, on the other hand, help to align your email marketing efforts with broader business objectives and ensure that you are targeting the right audience with the right messaging.

Which of the following is an example of a fundamental (s) of writing effective email copy.

Write for scannability.

Use the overall inbound tone.

Add preview text.

Have unsubscribe links.
Write for scannability.

A computer program that allows you to access and manage your email is the definition of what?

Email client

Email engagement

Email outsourcing

Email platform
Email client.
True or false? Your email outline is the actual template you’ll use for every email.

False. 

Your email outline is the general structure of your email, including the key sections and messages you want to include, but it is not the same as the actual template design. The email template is the actual visual format that your email will take, including the branding elements, layout, and formatting of the content. The outline provides a framework for the template design.
True or false? Analysis is only important in your inbound email marketing strategy.

False. 

Analysis is important in every aspect of email marketing, whether it's inbound or outbound. Analyzing metrics such as open rates, click-through rates, conversion rates, and ROI is crucial for understanding the effectiveness of your email campaigns and making data-driven decisions to optimize them. By analyzing the results of your email marketing efforts, you can identify what's working well and what's not, and adjust your strategy accordingly.

Your email metrics are your listening device to refine and improve your marketing emails over time. What can your metrics help you track?

Segmentation

Buyer’s journey

Conversations across emails

Social interaction

Conversations across emails. 

Your email metrics can help you track the effectiveness of your email campaigns, including how many people are opening, clicking, and converting from your emails. By analyzing these metrics, you can refine and improve your email marketing strategy over time, testing different approaches and optimizing your messaging to better engage your audience. Additionally, you can use email metrics to track conversations across emails, such as tracking how subscribers are interacting with your email series or tracking the success of a lead nurturing campaign. While metrics can indirectly inform segmentation and track the buyer's journey, they are primarily focused on tracking engagement and performance metrics. Social interactions are typically not tracked through email metrics.

There are three types of metrics you want to analyze and track. Two of them are individual email metrics and whole marketing channel metrics. What is the third?

Return on investment

Segmentation strategy

Social media collaboration

Return on value

Return on investment. 

The three types of metrics that are typically analyzed and tracked in email marketing are individual email metrics, whole marketing channel metrics, and return on investment (ROI). Individual email metrics track the performance of individual emails, such as open rates, click-through rates, and conversion rates. Whole marketing channel metrics track the overall performance of your email marketing program, such as total opens, clicks, and conversions across all emails. ROI metrics track the financial return on your email marketing investment, such as the revenue generated per email or the overall ROI of your email campaigns. Segmentation strategy and social media collaboration are not necessarily metrics, but rather tactics and strategies that can be used to optimize your email marketing efforts.

What is the definition of analysis?

The detailed examination of anything complex to understand its nature or to determine its essential features

The detailed examination of your emails specifically

The examination of all your marketing efforts at all times

The examination of how well you’re doing analysis

The detailed examination of anything complex to understand its nature or to determine its essential features. 

Analysis is a process of examining something in detail to better understand its nature, structure, or behavior. In the context of email marketing, analysis involves examining data and metrics associated with email campaigns to gain insights into their performance, such as open rates, click-through rates, conversion rates, and other key performance indicators. This analysis is used to understand the effectiveness of email campaigns, identify areas for improvement, and make data-driven decisions to optimize future email marketing efforts.

What does the send metric help you analyze?

Any email recipients who were sent to and accepted your message.

Any email recipients who can’t be sent to because you do not have the legal basis of permission to send to them under GDPR.

Any email recipients who can’t be sent to either because they unsubscribed, bounced, or were suppressed.

Any email recipients who have opted in to receive your message and were sent to.

Any email recipients who have opted in to receive your message and were sent to. 

The send metric tracks the total number of emails that were sent to your intended recipients, including those who have opted in to receive your messages and are on your subscriber list. This metric does not account for recipients who have opted out, bounced, or have been suppressed, as those emails either were not sent or were filtered out by your email service provider. The send metric is a key measurement for understanding the reach of your email campaigns and the potential audience that was exposed to your messaging.

The number of valid email addresses that accepted your company’s message is the definition of what metric?

Emails delivered

Emails sent

Emails suppressed

Emails rejected

Emails delivered. 

The emails delivered metric is the number of valid email addresses on your subscriber list that successfully received your email message. This metric does not account for those who have not yet received the message due to being filtered out by spam filters or other factors, but rather focuses on the number of messages accepted by recipients' email servers and delivered to their inbox. The delivered metric is an important measure of email deliverability, and can be used in conjunction with other metrics such as open rates and click-through rates to gain insights into email campaign performance.
